summary: Matched the 100-byte game-mode function `gmMainLib_8015FCC0` in `src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c`. The implementation initializes a newly declared global `gmMainLib_8046B0F0`, records whether `OSGetResetCode()` equals `0x80000000`, zeroes several integer fields, and stores `lbTime_8000AFBC()` into `x10`. The PR also corrected `gmMainLib_8015DA90`'s return type from `u32` to `int` in both the C file and header, and updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` to mark `func_8015FCC0` as OK. There were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Declared `struct gmMainLib_8046B0F0_t` with `bool x0` and integer fields `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, and `x14`, and defined the global `gmMainLib_8046B0F0`.

Implemented `void gmMainLib_8015FCC0(void)` as a reset/time-state initializer: it sets `x0` based on whether `OSGetResetCode() == 0x80000000`, clears `x4`, `x8`, and `xC`, and writes `lbTime_8000AFBC()` to `x10`.
Changed `gmMainLib_8015DA90`'s declared and defined return type from `u32` to `int`.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt`; the relevant new tracking line is `func_8015FCC0 = OK:0x8015FCC0`.
smart_moves: Used an explicit `if/else` assigning a local `bool val` before storing into `gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x0`, which is often necessary when matching compiler codegen for boolean comparisons instead of relying on a compact direct assignment.
Introduced the minimum local struct/global definition needed to represent the data touched by the matched function, avoiding broader speculative naming of the fields.
Kept the reset-code dependency explicit with the Dolphin OSReset header rather than relying on an undeclared external.
Synchronized the return-type correction for `gmMainLib_8015DA90` between implementation and header, preventing prototype drift.
decomp_lessons: For small initializer functions, matching may depend on representing bool stores and temporary locals exactly rather than writing the shortest equivalent C expression.
When a function touches an unknown global struct, it is acceptable to introduce placeholder field names like `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, etc. if the PR evidence does not justify semantic names.
Header prototypes should be updated when matching work reveals signedness or return-type details; here `gmMainLib_8015DA90` became `int` instead of `u32`.
Scratch/config updates can be noisy and large; future searches should focus on the actual source/header hunks plus the specific scratch line for the target address.
naming_conventions: `gmMainLib_8015FCC0` follows the project convention of retaining address-based names for unidentified game-mode functions.
The new global type name `gmMainLib_8046B0F0_t` and global `gmMainLib_8046B0F0` preserve address-based naming for unknown data.
Unknown struct fields use offset-style names such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, and `x14`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use an explicit local `bool val` plus `if/else` for `OSGetResetCode() == 0x80000000` when direct boolean assignment may not produce the same instructions.
Represent the reset magic constant exactly as `0x80000000`.
Initialize consecutive global struct fields with separate assignments rather than collapsing them, preserving likely store order.
Match dependency calls directly: `OSGetResetCode()` for reset state and `lbTime_8000AFBC()` for the time value.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type `gmMainLib_8046B0F0` fields semantically.
Search for other matched functions using `OSGetResetCode()` and the reset constant `0x80000000`.
Check whether later work gives `gmMainLib_8015FCC0` a non-address-based name.
Search for additional PRs correcting signed return types in `gmmain_lib.h`.
